title: Explain concurrent collections in Java. Why are they important?

answers.junior: Concurrent collections are thread-safe versions of collections like List and Map.
answers.middle: They allow safe access by multiple threads. Examples: ConcurrentHashMap, CopyOnWriteArrayList. Prevents data corruption.
answers.senior: Concurrent collections use internal locking or lock-free algorithms for thread safety and performance. Prefer them over synchronized collections for scalability.
answers.tricky: Use concurrent collections for high-performance multithreading. Analyze lock granularity and contention. Prefer lock-free designs for scalability. Monitor for subtle concurrency bugs.
